sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.7 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: Suppose that$P$is a finite 2-group. Does there exist a characteristic subgroup$L(P)$of$P$such that$L(P)$is normal in$H$for every finite group$H$that satisfies the following conditions: 1)$P$is a Sylow 2-subgroup of$H$, \, 2)~$H$is${ S}_4$-free, and 3)~$C_H(O_2(H)) O_2(H)$? { G.\,Glauberman} Yes, there does (B.\,Stellmacher, Israel J.~Math., { 94} (1996), 367--379).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
